items.title: Waarom niets mooier is dan een Armoedzaaier te zijn
items.summary: In de rubriek VI-Tijdmachine brengen we met regelmaat nieuwe verhalen over toen, en elke dag van het weekeinde herplaatsen we een artikelen uit ons rijke archief. Vanwege de opvallende titelrace die het haast onaantastbare Paris Saint-Germain momenteel uitvecht met RC Lens, gaan we terug naar 1998, als De Armoedzaaiers kampioen van Frankrijk worden.

items.title: Infantino: Resale will drive up WC ticket prices
items.summary: FIFA president Gianni Infantino said that many fans who have applied for tickets for the 2026 World Cup will be looking to sell them for profit on resale sites, thus driving up the cost of ticket prices.

items.title: Berman: Rodman deal shows new rule is working
items.summary: NWSL commissioner Jessica Berman cited Trinity Rodman's new deal as evidence that the league's new High Impact Player is working as intended: to retain and attract top players.

items.title: German official calls for World Cup boycott talks
items.summary: A German soccer federation executive committee member says it's time to consider a World Cup boycott because of the actions of U.S. President Donald Trump.

items.title: Sources: Arsenal exploring Álvarez summer move
items.summary: Arsenal are exploring the possibility of launching a summer move for Atletico Madrid forward Julián Álvarez, sources have told ESPN.

items.title: Laurens' weekend preview: Can Carrick stop Arsenal? Arbeloa faces another test, more
items.summary: What is Julien Laurens watching this weekend? Manchester United will look to upset Arsenal, Real Madrid face a tough test at Villarreal, and there's a race for second place in the WSL.

items.title: Tumult in Luik na nieuwe nederlaag Standard: personen dringen kleedkamer binnen
items.summary: Standard Luik leed vrijdagavond een ruime nederlaag tegen AA Gent en dat was voor diverse personen op de tribunes de druppel die de emmer deed overlopen. Relschoppers drongen de kleedkamer van Standard binnen, waarna stewards moesten ingrijpen.

items.title: 18-year-old tied with Scheffler for American Express lead after narrowly missing a 59
items.summary: Eighteen-year-old Blades Brown missed a final-hole six-foot putt to shoot an incredible 59 at the American Express Open, but still finished with a course record to tie Scottie Scheffler for the lead after two rounds.

items.summary: News from Friday’s press conferences, including Spurs, Chelsea, Manchester United and Aston Villa Continue reading...

items.title: Tottenham in talks to sign Andrew Robertson from Liverpool
items.summary: Liverpool open to selling player with five months on deal Desire among all parties to get transfer over the line Tottenham have made an approach to sign Andrew Robertson from Liverpool. The Premier League champions are open to allowing the long-serving left-back to leave, with only five months remaining on his contract. Liverpool respect Robertson’s wish to sort his long-term future as soon as possible. There is a desire on all sides to get a deal done quickly as Thomas Frank wrestles with major availability issues at Spurs. Continue reading...

items.title: Carrick's Man Utd aiming to 'prove' themselves as Arteta warns of 'lethal' counters
items.summary: Manchester United head coach Michael Carrick is relishing the opportunity for his side to prove themselves against league leaders Arsenal after a dream start at Old Trafford.

items.title: 'Chelsea wil verhuurde Juve-middenvelder, Barça jaagt op Nederlandse verdediger'
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: European football: Inter fight back to hit Pisa for six while Barcola lifts PSG top
items.summary: Inter recover from 2-0 down to go six clear in Serie A In Ligue 1, PSG go above Lens, who play on Saturday Inter were shocked to go two goals down at home to relegation-battling Pisa but fought back to earn a 6-2 win at San Siro on Friday, as they continue to set the pace at the top of Serie A. Stefano Moreo scored twice to put Pisa 2-0 up, the first thanks to a howler from the goalkeeper Yann Sommer, but Inter were ahead by the break. Piotr Zielinski converted a penalty and Lautaro Martínez and Francesco Pio Esposito both scored with headers in six minutes just before half-time. Continue reading...

items.title: ADO-coach hekelt spelopvatting Vitesse: 'Wij houden er niet van'
items.summary: ADO Den Haag-coach Robin Peter heeft niet genoten van de wedstrijd tegen Vitesse. Niet alleen door het resultaat, maar de Duitser stoorde zich ook aan het spel van de Arnhemmers.

items.title: Inter komt keepersfout al in eerste helft te boven tegen promovendus Pisa
items.summary: Internazionale heeft vrijdagavond een eenvoudige zege geboekt op promovendus Pisa. De koploper van Italië schrok in de eerste helft nog wel even na een fout van doelman Yann Sommer, maar liep daarna uit naar een ruime zege.

items.title: England fans' World Cup arrest record shows Infantino joke inaccurate
items.summary: Did Fifa president Gianni Infantino lean into stereotypes about England fans being arrested at the World Cup?

items.title: Deadly deliveries and controlled chaos: how Arsenal became set-piece masters
items.summary: Mikel Arteta’s side have scored 19 goals from corners this season. Why are their set pieces so effective and can they be stopped? Set pieces are dominating the Premier League this season, with almost 30% of goals coming from corners, free-kicks, penalties or long throws. The leaders, Arsenal, are kings of the dead ball, scoring 17 of their 40 league goals from set pieces (including penalties). But what makes Mikel Arteta’s side so effective in these areas, and what can opponents do to stop them? The data provides some answers. Continue reading...

items.title: Smotherman collapses at weigh-in for UFC 324 as fight cancelled
items.summary: A fight at UFC 324 has been cancelled after American fighter Cameron Smotherman collapsed during the weigh-in on Friday.

items.title: Euros abuse made me not want to leave hotel - Carter
items.summary: England defender Jess Carter says abuse of female footballers is getting worse and that she has had to step back from social media after receiving racist messages during Euro 2025.
